title: Changes to citizenship rules in 2020 - Canada.ca
content:
## Changes to citizenship rules in 2020
Amendments to the Citizenship Act in 2025 affect the first-generation limit to citizenship by descent. Depending on your situation, this section may not apply to you.
Learn about the most recent rules on citizenship by descent .
Before July 2020, only biological parents could pass down their Canadian citizenship to their children.
Biological parents have a genetic or gestational connection to their children.
As of July 2020, non-biological parents can pass down their Canadian citizenship to their children who were born outside Canada if they’re the child’s legal parent at birth (opens in a new tab) .
Non-biological parents have no genetic or gestational connection to their children.
It’s the birth certificate or record issued at the time of the child’s birth.
